chapters.title: How humanity has solved critical problems with science and technology
chapters.text:
Humans have come a long way in establishing themselves at the top of the food chain. But, as noted by Professor Harari, humans have always struggled to protect themselves from unfavorable conditions and factors that could cripple their growth.

And even though we got over some threats, others have historically forced the human race to the brink of collapse. Some of these threats include famine, poverty, and war, and they continue to remain focal conversations in our world even today. Prof. Yuval Noah Harari confirms that while there is originality in humanity’s travails, there is a lack of importance in how effective humans have become skilled in overcoming its nemesis mentioned above.

We do not become satisfied by leading a peaceful and prosperous existence. Instead, we become happy when reality matches our expectations. The bad news is that as conditions improve, expectations balloon. ~ Prof. Yuval Noah Harari

This response supersedes humans’ previous approach — blaming the chaos on an attempt by a deity or God to punish humankind. In the last few years, we have owned our failures, and we work relentlessly to learn from them, build infrastructures that would prevent future occurrences, and fixate on the next significant threat or challenge.

With the advent of technology, humanity has been able to contain more disasters and save more lives as well.

There is enough proof suggesting that the human race has successfully stopped the devastating effects of historical apocalyptic elements. However, with this success comes a vacuum that needs to get filled. What will humans do with all the knowledge and technology at their disposal when there are no new challenges or obstacles to tackle?

Usually, the human race will continue to stretch its limits and widen its horizons to find the next phase of its evolution. In this summary, Prof. Yuval Noah Harari hints that humans will probably base their next evolutionary quest on finding happiness, immortality, and divinity.

chapters.title: The things that will drive humanity’s future existence
chapters.text:
Humanity will seek to establish immortality, happiness, and divinity in the future. Although this reality is still several years away, the world we live in today has begun to lay the foundation for it. This is why doctors tell patients all their health conditions. Billions of dollars have been invested in creating new drugs and improving the efficiency of nanotechnology and other technologies that can give humans an edge over death.

Not surprisingly, this nanotech treatment that would have been met with criticism is now finding its way into the heart of the technology sphere as tech giants continue to introduce welcomed initiatives and research with the main aim of eradicating death. It comes as no surprise that humanity has been able to buff up its life expectancy from 40, recorded in the eras when plagues, famine, and war ran riot, to 70 in the 20th century. However, improving our chances against death might not necessarily mean a complete escape from fatalities resulting from suicide and accidents.

The quest to find happiness has caused an almost obsessive innovative drive in recent years.

Scientists and researchers are going past creating drugs that induce happiness biochemically. They are also interested in finding ways to send the right electrical stimuli to the brain to trigger and motivate happiness, genetically reengineering the happiness process.

Humans’ inability to sustain a happy state is already fueling a new horde of psychological enhancement drugs and technologies like antidepressants. And humans will find the ultimate source of pleasure sensations.

The implications of humans’ pursuit of immortality and happiness naturally culminate in establishing humans as divine creatures, a creature that is void of pain, fear, or flaws. This reality is what Professor Yuval Noah Harari calls the transformation of Homo Sapiens to Homo Deus.

In this reality, humanity is not trying to acquire the same level of power and knowledge symbolic of the biblical God. Instead, humans will upgrade their capacity to resemble mythological deities like Zeus, who could control environmental elements, command creativity, and challenge death.

The fusing of man and machine will cause the altering of genes. The creation of intelligent robots will permanently change the world, defy the evolution timeline, and change history.

chapters.title: Domestication defines the relationship between humans, plants, and animals, and the changing ecological landscape
chapters.text:
Having established humanity’s dominance and our influence on the world more than ever, we must discover humanity’s current course by checking history. Before humans uncovered the concept of evolution from animals, particularly reptiles, many religious accounts of creation confirmed that human beings were specially made to govern the Earth’s affairs.

Science gave humans the power and confidence to make changes.

Archeologists have discovered that humans started as hunters and gatherers, not the agriculturist era that Christianity, Judaism, and Hinduism documented. Before the agricultural revolution, there was an animalist’s era where humans maintained a balance between their relationship with animals and plants. Humans acknowledged that animals and plants contributed to their ecosystem’s well-being, so they interacted with them.

On the other hand, with the help of religion, the agricultural revolution concluded that man and God/gods were the core entities that kept the world working. As such, this age relegated and belittled animals as soulless and lesser forms of life, whose role was to feed humans and appease God/gods.

As a result of human’s inquisitive nature, man’s and gods’ age slowly gave room for an era that elevated humans to God’s status as humans asserted utter dominance over the world. In this era, science has given humanity the ability to reshape its surroundings and make life easier. As a result, domestication continues to define the relationship between humans, animals, and plants.

Our task must be to free ourselves by widening our circle of compassion to embrace all living creatures and the whole of nature and its beauty. ~ Albert Einstein

According to Professor Yuval Noah Harari’s stats, the number of domesticated animals is far higher than the number of animals roaming the wild. Humanity is the only factor that changed this planet’s ecology at a fast rate. Our actions have led to extinctions of rivals, shifting of tectonic plates, asteroid collisions, and climate change.

These happenings are because humans believe that their lives are more important than that of other species. It is a function of religious and unproven assertions that only humans have souls or consciousness that could enable them to access an afterlife. At the same time, animals would supposedly fade into nothingness. However, it is a bit wrong to imply that animals lack a conscious mind.

chapters.title: The world that humans have built today has its foundation in myths
chapters.text:
As humans realized all that could get accomplished simply by putting their imagination to work, it became apparent that there was only one possible outcome. This process started about 70,000 years ago when tribes and clans used stories to ensure their people cooperate. These stories, beliefs, and superstitions made people not break ranks. They motivated people to do all it took to protect the values of their community.

As efficient and excellent as this approach was, the isolation and separation of sapiens and the inability to feed thousands of people by hunting or gathering did limit its efficiency. It wasn’t until the emergence of the agricultural era that human’s imaginative capacity showed positive results. This concept was made manifest in ancient Egypt, where Pharaoh was merely a symbol of power or divinity. Regardless of who was in control, the engines of this civilization were officials that ensured the citizens continued to believe.

Imagination is the only weapon in the war against reality. ~ Lewis Carroll

As such, people rely on forming compelling myths whenever they want to bring other people together to share one goal. The use of written texts also made all the difference, allowing humans to bend reality and motivate people’s loyalty. This assertion is evident in the values of the holy scriptures and how much they have influenced humans’ actions for thousands of years.

Religion continues to evolve based on the needs of society and continues to help humanity make the right decisions.

While religions sought to create structures and regulations to govern humans’ behaviors, science, which some call the modern myth, looks to separate facts and findings from ethics. Science also empowers us to find or create solutions for plagues, famine, and war. As for religion, it leans towards creating an order. Both science and religion have brought humans far, and they will continue to push humanity to make choices that indeed count.

chapters.title: The undeniable influence of economic growth on the environment
chapters.text:
The modern-day world thrived with the unleashing of human’s thirst for power and growth. Even though this contradicts the stagnant economic philosophies that ruled the premodern world, it is highly regarded today as humans continue to destroy old institutions for new ones.

Modernity generally revolves around a notion that humankind, through knowledge and ideas, can create new resources and grow its wealth.

On the contrary, the old world relied on a more passive model that suggests that humanity had access to lots of raw materials and energy. Its capacity to maximize these resources determines its survival. To ensure that humans stick to this law of maximizing resources, humanity erected religious institutions. These institutions began preaching that greed would lead to doom and destruction.

However, in recent times, these institutions are no longer healthy; humans have evolved into creatures that believe that they have a faster and better solution to all of humanity’s problems. But these come at a price. The rapid speed of modern economic growth has its disadvantages. First, the human environment cannot sustain such a level of productivity. For every policy that brings about change or every scientific breakthrough recorded, the planet moves closer to the brink of collapse.

Considering the effects of human-made activities on Earth’s ecology, one would expect that leaders would take stricter routes in curbing excesses. But the reverse is the case. Maybe the leaders are unaware of the problem’s critical nature as they continue to postpone actions. Or perhaps those that genuinely believe in growth and development are certain that science would cover up for them.

chapters.title: The belief system for future generations
chapters.text:
According to Professor Harari, Humanism is any religion that elevates humankind to god-like status. Some societies believe that humans, not God, can direct and decide the course of nature. They believe that humanity is the only factor that could give meaning to life. Nations now value their citizens’ emotions or thoughts, resulting in a free market that understands the role and power of consumers.

Humanism, especially liberalism, relies on a phenomenon that individual humans have the wisdom to make sound choices. This view has improved and strengthened today’s leading political argument that suggests that voters and customers have the final say in governance and the economy. Although this sentiment is the current trend, Professor Yuval Noah Harari suggests that free will is nothing but a lie.

The human mind is a network of neurons triggered continuously and pushed to translate sensations into emotions.

Scientists have suggested that, in most cases, our minds determine our actions. Furthermore, Professor Yuval Noah Harari argues that humans consist of 2 cognitive parts – the narrative self and the experiencing self. The experiencing self takes a record of your experiences, while the narrative self uses these experiences to tell a story or make big decisions. In its quest to make meaning of your experiences, the narrative self would censor some aspect of our experiences and base its conclusion on the average of the peak and bottom moments.

Life aims to live it, taste experience to the utmost, and reach out eagerly and without fear for a newer and richer experience. ~ Eleanor Roosevelt

In other words, the collaboration of these selves is how we translate experiences and create our beliefs, which would naturally determine our choices. Therefore, your life decisions depend on how much of your adventures you can retain.

Liberalism may not have all the answers for future generations, as human intelligence now competes with non-organic algorithms. Already, we see this prediction manifest in various industries, with skeptics criticizing automation and how it threatens human jobs. Sooner rather than later, artificial intelligence will replace humans in almost every profession.

Although this notion suggests that humans will become jobless and useless in the future, Professor Yuval Noah Harari argues that humanity’s individuality will not become extinct. Humans will continue to remain relevant.

chapters.title: Techno-humanism and data doctrine is poised to govern the third millennium
chapters.text:
Two distinct religions would guide humanity into its explosive technology future: Techno-humanism and Dataism. Techno-humanism seeks to maintain humanism by introducing improvement elements. These improvements will aid humanity’s quest for divinity by evolving from Homo Sapiens to Homo Deus — a superior human species

Homo sapiens or humans resulted from the genetic altering of animals, and this process took millions of years. Even as the homo sapiens have created a new world, they must evolve to the next phase to continue the innovation cycle. Herein lies the fundamentals of techno-humanism.

It is not the strongest or the most intelligent who will survive but those who can best manage change. ~ Leon C. Megginson

However, how will we ensure that biological alterations would not rob us of our will if we are so keen on sustaining? Since nanotechnology and bionic parts will influence mental states in different ways, it is easy to totally key in this process and forever be a slave to enhancements that could shape our biology. Dataism claims that humans do not have the right processing power to manage the new world. Dataism believes that future systems’ control should fall in the hands of intelligent machines that can process the tremendous amount of data generated.

Humanity has always created otherworldly explanations for happenings greater than their own knowledge, hence the advent of religion.

Already, this prophecy is becoming evident. Governments are clueless, so are corporations. Humans are slowly introducing one form of the super processor to help source data and utilize it. However, Dataism is not a religion against humans; instead, humans will rely on the systems of Dataism to separate good from bad, bringing about the relationship between God and man and the religious system that spoke about this.

Did you know? Dataism as a term was first used in a 2013 New York Times piece by David Brooks.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Professor Yuval Noah Harari is a firm believer in the future of humanity and how big the role technology is poised to play in it. Change is constant, and humanity has survived this long because it is always ready to adapt to change. Humanity makes up explanations for these changes that are natural disasters and not the act of an angered god. Harari cites that religion is man’s way of either explaining or evading change.

As change is constant, so is the possibility of more religions popping up. The new belief system is science and technology. Humanity, however, can be resistant to change and the advent of science garnered a lot of resistance. Harari argues that science, however, is the only religion to create and sustain humanity’s development.

The engine of human survival is the ability to always adapt to its surroundings and even go as far as dominating it.

Many models that have worked for humanity will get dumped as humans transit into the next millennium. Scarier is how much technology and knowledge will empower society to shed all its flaws to attain divinity. When this time comes, war, poor health, famine, terrible economy, and plagues will lose their position as humans’ greatest nemesis. Instead of them, humanity will contend with far more significant threats.

We do not become satisfied by leading a peaceful and prosperous existence. Rather, we become satisfied when reality matches our expectations. The bad news is that as conditions improve, expectations balloon. ~ Prof. Yuval Noah Harari

Try this

Technology holds great promise in just about every facet of human existence; medicine, sports, and even housing. It would be beneficial if we get acquainted with the religion of dataism. Start by learning all you can about data and how it shapes the world.
